Petition To Approve: Compromise Of Disputed Claim (Minor)
Off Calendar Jan-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Alexis S. McKenna, Esq. appeared with petitioner Johathan W. Trawinski, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Jonnie Row, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Order appointing Mr. Trawinski as Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed on May 15, 2015.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $250,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $82,500.00.
Medical Bills - $20,000.00.
Costs - $53,490.63.

The net balance of $94,009.37 shall be disbursed as follows: $30,000.00 shall be transferred to a custodian (Jonathan M. Trawinski) for the benefit of the minor under the California Uniform Transfer to Minors Act (CUTMA). These funds being placed into the CUTMA are intended to be used for treatment and medication for the minor, and to purchase a vehicle for him when his is over 16 years old. The remainder shall go to the minor when he turns 18 years old; and $64,009.37 shall be invested in a single-premium deferred annuity, with a $25,000.00 guaranted lump sum payment on 02/19/2028 and a $67,365.00 guaranteed lump sum payment on 02/19/2033.

Order Approving the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to February 27, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the Status of the purchase of the single-premium deferred annuity.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JONNIE ROE'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant San Francisco Unified School District is GRANTED in part, as follows:
1 The parties are directed to negotiate a protective order providing that information disclosed in discovery shall be kept confidential and not used for any purpose other than the instant litigation, or present alternative forms of such an order, within 10 days of this order.
2. Following entry of the protective order, Defendants shall respond to Form Interrogatory 12.1 by providing the full names of student witnesses, and contact addresses and phone numbers for their parents.
3. The motion is granted with respect to Document Requests 3, 5, 7, 9, 17, 21-22, 25-26, 29-30, 33-36, 46, 48, 53, 58, 67-69, and 71. Objections to said requests are overruled, it being noted that no Brown Act documents are being sought.
